name: Chocolate Chestnut Bourbon Torte W/a Kentucky
recipeIngredient: 6 tablespoons Chilled butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the cake: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 C). Grease and flour two nine-inch cake pans. Beat egg yolks well. Stir in sugar and bourbon, then blend in chestnut puree. Stir in ground pecans. Whip egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff. Fold whites into batter. Divide mixture between the two cake pans and bake for 25 minutes. Cool on a rack, then remove from pans.
2. Make the filling: Beat 2 egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar. Add instant coffee and hot water. Beat chilled butter into mixture, then add bourbon and remaining chestnut puree. Stir in chopped bitter chocolate. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
3. Melt bittersweet chocolate with heavy cream over low heat. Stir in 4 tablespoons bourbon, transfer to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
4. To assemble cake: Spread chilled chestnut filling atop one cake layer and cover with the other. Cover torte with chocolate icing and sprinkle with chopped pecan pieces. Chill before serving. Meanwhile, prepare bourbon chantilly: Whip 2 cups cream to soft peaks, gradually beat in confectioners' sugar, then fold in 4 tablespoons bourbon.
From Chicago Tribune Magazine (cooking section)11-14-93. Featured was Cathy Cary, who with her husband, Will, runs a catering business (La Peche) and a 94 seat restaurant (Lilly's) in Louisville, KY. This issue's feature centered around buffet items for holiday parties.
